primary_sources.relevance: PerformanceEventTiming groups related trusted input events through non-zero interactionId and exposes separable input, processing, and presentation latency components.
Long Animation Frames entries provide retained main-thread congestion evidence that remains distinct from interaction correlation and end-user claim truth.

finding: A combined interaction evidence packet must bind both classifier verdicts to one immutable deployment, exact source commit, target device/browser, observation window, and ordered interaction sequence while preserving each verdict and its digest independently.
design_implication: Vercel Studio can now assemble and verify one fail-closed packet after running the existing Event Timing and Long Animation Frames classifiers. Unsupported instrumentation, non-accepting verdicts, deployment mismatch, missing device context, or post-build tampering prevent acceptance.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical source before commit

evidence_strength: committed implementation plus deterministic local tests; no browser, device, or deployment execution in this run
claim_limits: interactionId correlation is page-lifecycle local and does not prove causality
Long Animation Frames evidence does not prove universal smoothness
packet acceptance is scoped to the exact retained deployment and observation context
SHA-256 integrity is not producer authentication or proof that a human-visible outcome occurred
falsification_route: Repeat the exact ordered interaction sequence against the same immutable deployment on the target device; reject the packet if deployment identity changes, either classifier is unsupported or non-accepting, observation context is incomplete, or any retained digest changes.
